target.title: test(auto-reply): add unit tests for ACP reset target session key resolution
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The `acp-reset-target.ts` module resolves ACP reset targets from sessions and command directives, coordinating service bindings, configured bindings, and raw ACP binding fallback. Despite being a core path in auto-reply reset target resolution, this module had no unit tests to verify the resolution logic.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add unit tests for `resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey` to verify:
- Input normalization edge cases (missing channel/conversationId, empty strings, non-ACP active sessions)
- Service binding resolution (ACP key recognition, non-ACP filtering, whitespace trimming, empty-after-trim)
- Priority chain: service binding takes precedence over active session
- `skipConfiguredFallbackWhenActiveSessionNonAcp` flag behavior (skip vs continue)
- Configured binding resolution with `allowNonAcpBindingSessionKey` flag
- Raw configured ACP session key fallback from bindings list
- `fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound` default/disabled behavior
- accountId resolution from channel `defaultAccount` config

This improves test coverage and prevents regressions when the function is modified.

## User Impact

ACP reset target resolution now has dedicated unit coverage, reducing the risk of regressions when the function is modified.

## Evidence

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds direct coverage for existing helper behavior rather than reporting a user-visible runtime bug. Source inspection confirms the helper behavior the tests target.

Direct behavior probe (no mocks — real function exercised through default dependencies):

```text
$ node --import tsx -e "import('./src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.js').then(({ resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey }) => { const cfg = { channels: {}, bindings: [] }; const results = []; results.push({ desc: 'no channel/conversationId returns undefined', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg }) }); results.push({ desc: 'active ACP session preserved without channel', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg, activeSessionKey: 'acp:real-session' }), expected: 'acp:real-session' }); results.push({ desc: 'non-ACP active session returns undefined without channel', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg, activeSessionKey: 'non-acp' }) }); results.push({ desc: 'fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound=false blocks fallback', result: resolveEffectiveResetTargetSessionKey({ cfg, channel: 'test', conversationId: 'test-c', activeSessionKey: 'acp:test', fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound: false }) }); console.log(JSON.stringify(results, null, 2)); })"
[
  {
    "desc": "no channel/conversationId returns undefined"
  },
  {
    "desc": "active ACP session preserved without channel",
    "result": "acp:real-session",
    "expected": "acp:real-session"
  },
  {
    "desc": "non-ACP active session returns undefined without channel"
  },
  {
    "desc": "fallbackToActiveAcpWhenUnbound=false blocks fallback"
  }
]
```

Targeted test:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
   Start at  23:48:24
   Duration  2.49s (transform 997ms, setup 0ms, import 2.25s, tests 17ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 14.36s
```

Formatting:

```text
$ npx oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.ts src/auto-reply/reply/acp-reset-target.test.ts
Checking formatting...

All matched files use the correct format.
```

Whitespace:

```text
$ git diff --check
```

## AI-assisted

Prepared with Codex. I reviewed the change, understand the touched code path, and kept the PR focused on the test coverage described above.
target.author: zenglingbiao

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
